channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Man rescued from rubble in Myanmar's capital but hope fading of finding more earthquake survivors
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Rescue crews in Myanmar pulled a 26-year-old man out alive from the rubble of the capital city hotel where he worked early Wednesday, but most teams were finding only bodies five days after a massive earthquake hit the country.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israel’s military operation in Gaza Strip expanding to seize 'large areas,' defense minister says
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel’s military operation in the Gaza Strip is expanding to seize “large areas,” the defense minister said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Danish prime minister heads to Greenland as Trump seeks control of the Arctic territory
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — Danish Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen is traveling to Greenland on Wednesday for a three-day trip aimed at building the trust of Greenlandic officials at a time that the Trump administration is seeking control of the vast Arctic territory.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: As Israel advances in Gaza, many exhausted families flee again. Some can't bear it
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — As Israel orders wide new evacuations across the Gaza Strip, Palestinians say they are crushed by exhaustion and hopelessness at the prospect of fleeing once again. Many are packing a few belongings and trudging off in search of new shelters.
item.dc:creator: FATMA KHALED and MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Survivors still being found from Myanmar earthquake, but hopes begin to fade as deaths exceed 2,700
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Rescue workers saved a 63-year-old woman from the rubble of a building in Myanmar's capital on Tuesday, but hope was fading of finding many more survivors of the violent earthquake that killed more than 2,700 people, compounding a humanitarian crisis caused by a civil war.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A wary Europe awaits Rubio with NATO's future on the line
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is traveling this week to a gathering of top diplomats from NATO countries.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee And Lorne Cook, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Suspected US airstrikes in Yemen kill at least 4 people near Hodeida, Houthi rebels say
item.description: Suspected U.S. airstrikes have battered rebel-controlled areas of Yemen, with the Houthis saying one strike killed at least four people near the Red Sea port city of Hodeida.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Paraguay recalls ambassador to Brazil and suspends dam talks over espionage revelations
item.description: ASUNCIÓN, Paraguay (AP) — Paraguay announced Tuesday that it was recalling its ambassador to Brazil a day after Brazilian authorities acknowledged that their country's intelligence agency spied on Paraguayan officials in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bipartisan senators introduce primary, secondary Russia sanctions
item.description: Sens. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) and Richard Blumenthal (D-Conn.) introduced legislation on Tuesday seeking to impose primary and secondary sanctions against Russia if it does not agree to long lasting peace with its neighboring country, Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Mexico's security chief quietly forms an elite force to take on the drug cartels
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Six years ago Mexico’s president disbanded the country’s Federal Police and handed security responsibilities fully to the military. Now, his successor has quietly begun to build an elite civilian investigative and special operations force to fight the drug cartels.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA VERZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Most in survey of Europeans support retaliatory tariffs against US
item.description: Most Western Europeans are supportive of the continent’s intent to fire back when President Trump moves forward with tariffs set to take effect Wednesday, according to a survey released on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: At just 29, Jordan Bardella inherits the French far-right spotlight, whether he’s ready or not
item.description: PARIS (AP) — He wears his suits like armor, smiles like a pop star and boasts more than 2 million followers on TikTok.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Aftershock from a political 'earthquake' as Le Pen barred from presidential run in 2027
item.description: Le Pen called her ban from running a "nuclear bomb" by the system, after a court convicted her of embezzlement and blocked her from office for five years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EU prepared to retaliate against Trump tariffs, von der Leyen says
item.description: The European Union (EU) is prepared to retaliate against President Trump’s existing and upcoming tariffs, although the trade bloc is open to negotiations with Washington, according to European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Survivors still being found from Burma earthquake, but hopes begin to fade as deaths exceed 2,700
item.description: Burma was hit by a 7.7 magnitude earthquake on Friday, killing more than 2,700 people, compounding a humanitarian crisis caused by a civil war. Thailand also sustained damage.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A fireball from a burst gas pipeline in Malaysia injures 145 people
item.description: KUALA LUMPUR, Malaysia (AP) — A fireball that erupted from a burst gas pipeline soared into the sky outside Malaysia's largest city and injured 145 people as it burned for several hours before being put out, authorities said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Germany sees Ukraine truce efforts as deadlocked, while China says the talks are encouraging
item.description: Germany’s chief diplomat is describing U.S. President Donald Trump’s efforts to secure a truce in the three-year war between Russia and Ukraine as deadlocked.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israeli military orders the evacuation of Gaza's southern city of Rafah
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel's military on Monday issued sweeping evacuation orders covering Rafah and nearby areas, indicating it could soon launch another major ground operation in the Gaza Strip's southernmost city.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran's Khamenei warns of 'strong blow' as Trump threatens to drop bombs, Putin silent on US ire
item.description: Iran and Russia took decisively different approaches in response to President Donald Trump's threats as Tehran vowed action against any "mischief" while the Kremlin remains tight-lipped.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli forces order evacuation for most of Rafah ahead of attack on the area
item.description: The Israeli military issued evacuation orders on Monday for most of Rafah and suggested it may soon launch another ground operation in the city.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran has rejected direct negotiations with the US in response to Trump's letter
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Iran's president said Sunday that the Islamic Republic rejected direct negotiations with the United States over its rapidly advancing nuclear program, offering Tehran's first response to a letter that U.S.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and AMIR VAHDAT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Smell of death permeates Myanmar cities after quake kills over 1,600, leaves others buried
item.description: The 7.7 magnitude quake hit midday Friday with an epicenter near Mandalay, bringing down scores of buildings and damaging other infrastructure like the city’s airport.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump threatens to bomb Iran unless they end nuclear weapons program and begin talks on new deal
item.description: Trump warned Iran after sending a letter to revive nuclear talks. With "maximum pressure" back on the table, tensions between the two nations continue to rise.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian attack on hospital, shopping center leaves 2 dead as Ukraine braces for fresh offensive
item.description: A Russian drone attack killed two people in Kharkiv on Sunday amid a fresh offensive ahead of peace talks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Smell of death permeates Myanmar cities after quake kills over 1,600 and leaves countless buried
item.description: MANDALAY, Myanmar (AP) — The smell of decaying bodies permeated the streets of Myanmar's second-largest city on Sunday as people worked frantically by hand to clear rubble in the hope of finding someone still alive, two days after a massive earthquake struck that killed more than 1,600 people and le
item.dc:creator: THEIN ZAW, DAVID RISING and GRANT PECK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hegseth announces plan for US command upgrades in Japan to deter China
item.description: U.S. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth announced a plan to upgrade the U.S. military command in Japan, an "indispensable" country against Chinese aggression.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Countries sending humanitarian aid after Myanmar earthquake
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The death toll from Myanmar's powerful 7.7 magnitude earthquake keeps climbing amid rescue efforts. The military government said Saturday that 1,644 people have been killed, with thousands of others injured and dozens missing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Myanmar's earthquake death toll jumps to 1,644 as more bodies are recovered from the rubble
item.description: Myanmar’s ruling military confirmed death toll from a devastating 7.7 magnitude earthquake rose to 1,644, as more bodies were pulled from the rubble of the scores of buildings that collapsed.
item.dc:creator: David Rising, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Doctor cites the pope's 'surprising improvement' after surviving life-threatening crises
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis has shown ‘’a truly surprising improvement’’ since returning to the Vatican to convalesce after surviving a life-threatening bout with double-pneumonia, the doctor who coordinated the pontiff’s five-week hospitalization said Saturday. “I find him very lively,’’ Dr.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Massive quake rocks Myanmar and Thailand. Hundreds feared dead
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A powerful earthquake rocked Myanmar on Friday, causing extensive damage across a wide swath of one of the world’s poorest countries and prompting officials to warn that the initial death toll — above 140 — was likely to grow in the days ahead.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING and JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Bali expands tourist guidelines over misbehavior from travelers
item.description: Rules include shelling out Bali's tourist levy — equivalent to $9 USD — and wearing "modest and respectful clothing" in public.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Powerful earthquake rocks Thailand and Myanmar, kills more than 150
item.description: A 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocked Thailand and neighboring Myanmar midday on Friday, burying an unknown number when a high-rise building under construction collapsed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Suspected US strikes pummel Houthi-controlled areas of Yemen
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes pummeled sites across Yemen controlled by the Houthi rebels early Friday, including neighborhoods in the capital, Sanaa.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: King Charles III briefly hospitalized for observation following side effects from cancer treatment
item.description: Charles' cancer diagnosis has heaped pressure on the British monarchy, which is still evolving after the 70-year reign of the late Queen Elizabeth II.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: New 2-clawed dinosaur discovered in Mongolia
item.description: Although the new species has large front claws, it is likely a herbivore who used them to grab leaves.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Signal leak: Trump adviser Mike Waltz may have to go, Warner says
item.description: “There needs to be accountability,” said Sen. Warner, who faults Trump's inner circle with a number of security gaffes.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: The Atlantic releases full Signal text chain
item.description: The Atlantic editor-in-chief on Wednesday released the full text message chain between Trump administration officials.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation
